distractor_plausibility.notes: All 15 distractors across 5 MCQ are plausible beginner misconceptions grounded in 14.8 concepts. q1 distractors conflate irreversibility with cost, tool-count, and probabilistic output — recognisable confusions. q2 distractors confuse HITL with retry loops, passive logging, and time-delay safety. q3 distractors present plausible but incorrect agent failure characterisations. q4 distractors represent the three wrong autonomy-spectrum choices a beginner might pick. q5 distractors plausibly flag read-only tools or over-apply checkpoints. 0 absurd options identified.

gift_format.notes: SOFT: Six '// LO...' comment lines present in the .gift file (lines 3, 12, 21, 30, 39, 48). GIFT syntax allows '//' comments and parsers will ignore them, but the pipeline spec states 'No metadata comments in the .gift file — meta content in quiz.meta.json only.' These lines duplicate LO labels already captured in quiz.meta.json per_question[].objective_ref. They do not break parsing but violate the pipeline rule. $CATEGORY line present. All 5 MCQ have = correct with #feedback, ~ distractors with #feedback, and #### general feedback. q6 uses {} essay format — acceptable. No structural GIFT errors.

The LO labels are already captured in quiz.meta.json per_question[].objective_ref. Recommend removing these comment lines in a future revision to keep metadata out of the .gift file.
